name: Asparagus and Chicken in Black Bean Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinating:
- With a sharp paring knife, scrape chicken meat from the thighs and slice into thin strips across the grain.
- (Breast meat is not preferred for this dish, as the meat is too dry and spongy.)
- Combine 1 tablespoon of sherry, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, 1 teaspoon of cornstarch, and 1 teaspoon of sugar in a bowl.
- Massage the liquid into the chicken with your fingers. Marinate for 15 to 30 minutes.
2. Sauce Preparation:
- Rinse asparagus and peel the tough white outer skin off the ends.
- Slice the asparagus diagonally into 2 1/2-inch sections.
- Rinse fermented black beans.
- In a bowl, combine mashed black beans, minced garlic, brown sugar, black soy sauce, and chicken stock. Stir until well combined. Set aside for at least 15 minutes.
3. Stir Frying:
- Add 2/3 of the oil to a hot wok. When the oil is very hot, add the chicken.
- Stir-fry the chicken on high heat for about 3 minutes or until it begins to shrink and firm up. Remove the chicken to a holding bowl.
4. Reheat Wok:
- Increase the heat in the wok to high and add the remaining oil.
- When the oil is hot, add the black bean sauce.
- Stir-fry for 1 minute. Add the asparagus and mix with the sauce.
- Continue stirring until the sauce boils and coats the asparagus. If there is still too much liquid, reduce the sauce further before returning the ingredients to coat them evenly.
5. Serving:
- Serve in individual portions when the asparagus turns bright green.
